commit.message:
fix(offline): comportamiento offline completo Dynamic UI (G-01..G-07) (#51)

* fix(offline): emit DataDeleted/DataChanged regardless of null data (G-01)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(offline): validate enqueue return + invalidate cache on offline ops (G-07/G-09/G-10)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(offline): add SyncEvents system, failed mutations feedback, debounce (G-02/G-05)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(offline): PUT/PATCH deduplication by endpoint + mutation TTL (G-03/G-06)

G-03: PUT/PATCH mutations with same endpoint+method now replace body
instead of being rejected as duplicates (last-write-wins).
G-06: Mutations older than 7 days are removed during sync processing.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(offline): silent 204 body + auto-navigate back on delete

- RemoteDataLoader: skip JSON parse when response body is empty (204 No
  Content on DELETE), eliminates spurious warning log
- DynamicScreen: call onBack() before showSnackbar() so detail screen
  navigates to list immediately after delete instead of waiting for
  snackbar to dismiss

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(offline): address Copilot review comments (G-01..G-07)

MutationQueue:
- Check findExistingForReplace before maxMutations: replace doesn't
  need extra capacity so must not be rejected on full queue
- Reset retryCount=0 and status=PENDING when replacing PUT/PATCH body
- Update _failedCount in clearImmediate() and restoreFromStorage()

SyncEngine:
- Track conflictEventsJob separately; cancel it in stop() to prevent
  leaked collectors on restart
- Include CONFLICTED mutations in terminal state check so engine no
  longer reports Completed when conflicted items remain
- Cancel both jobs at start of start() before re-creating them

ConnectivitySyncManager:
- Cancel reconnectDebounceJob in stop() and at start of start() to
  prevent stale reconnect jobs running after stop/restart

RemoteDataLoader:
- Return Success(JsonObject(emptyMap())) for 204/empty body instead of
  Success(null); reserves null for "queued offline" semantics only
- Rename local var body→responseBody to avoid shadowing parameter

DynamicScreenViewModel:
- Only emit DataChanged when result.data != null (mutation executed);
  avoid triggering list reloads while still offline
- DataDeleted emission unchanged: optimistic delete is intended (G-01)

DynamicScreen:
- Revert snackbar/onBack order so message is shown before navigating
  back; calling onBack first cancels the scope, silencing the snackbar

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(offline): address remaining Copilot review comments (PR #51)

MutationQueue:
- Include CONFLICTED in failedCount (persistAndNotify + restoreFromStorage)
  so failedCount matches the semantics of SyncEngine.HasFailedMutations

SyncEngine:
- Inject Clock (default Clock.System) to make TTL checks testable
- Use BufferOverflow.DROP_OLDEST on _syncEvents to prevent emit() from
  suspending when no collector is active
- Replace for(snapshot)+dequeue() loop with while(dequeue()!=null) so
  TTL checks and processing always operate on the same item
- Fix early return when pending is empty: surface FAILED/CONFLICTED as
  HasFailedMutations instead of always returning Idle (handles app-restart
  case where only terminal mutations remain in the queue)

RemoteDataLoader:
- Return Failure instead of Success(null) on JSON parse error
- Return JsonObject(emptyMap()) instead of null when cast to JsonObject
  fails; null is reserved for "queued offline" semantics only

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(workspace): interactive directory explorer with skill detection and WSL support

Add `ahub workspace register` command with interactive filesystem browser
that auto-detects well-known agent skill directories:

- Claude Code (.claude/commands, .claude/skills, .claude/agents, .claude/prompts)
- Codex (.codex/skills, .codex/agents, .codex/prompts)
- Cursor (.cursor/rules, .cursor/agents, .cursor/prompts)
- Windsurf (.windsurf/rules), Aider (.aider/conventions)
- Cline (.cline/rules), Continue (.continue/prompts)
- Generic (.skills)

Features:
- Interactive directory navigation with inquirer prompts
- Auto-scan shows detected skill dirs with item counts and tool badges
- Bulk or selective registration of detected directories
- WSL path resolution (\wsl.localhost\Ubuntu\... <-> /home/...)
- Suggested starting directories based on common workspace locations
- Manual path entry fallback
- Sub-commands: register, list, active, unregister

New modules:
- src/core/wsl.ts: WSL detection, UNC path conversion, normalization
- src/core/explorer.ts: WELL_KNOWN_SKILL_DIRS, scanForSkillDirs, listDirectory
- src/cli/commands/workspace.ts: Full interactive CLI with sub-commands

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: outbox flush + server resp_buf — fix message loss for large blobs (v0.9.16 / v0.5.8) [BUILD]

Three fixes for message delivery reliability:

1. Outbox race condition (messenger): Replace per-message DHT PUT with
   flush-only approach. messenger_flush_recipient_outbox() reads ALL
   undelivered messages from messages.db, re-encrypts each, serializes
   into a single blob, and PUTs once. Eliminates read-modify-write race
   when CLI spawns separate process per send.

2. Server response buffer (nodus): resp_buf was 64KB static — values
   >55KB data caused silent GET failures (CBOR encoder overflow, no
   response sent). Increased to NODUS_MAX_VALUE_SIZE + 64KB (~1.1MB).
   Also fixed replicate_value() buffer (same 64KB limit).

3. Error handling: handle_t2_get() and handle_t2_get_all() now check
   nodus_t2_result() return value and send error response instead of
   silently dropping.

Tested: 10 rapid messages chip→punk, all 10 delivered.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: sandbox-exec whitelist profile for macOS 26+

(allow default) no longer overrides subsequent deny rules on macOS 26.
Switch to (deny default) + explicit whitelist approach: allow all
non-file-write operations, then whitelist specific writable paths.

from writing to main repo while worktree writes succeed.

commit.message:
fix: preserve inline code spans during HTML stripping and tag extraction

Two bugs caused garbled tags in the registry manifest:

1. clean.ts: HTML/JSX tag stripping regex destroyed content inside
   inline code backticks (e.g. `<MyButton />` became empty ``).
   Fixed by protecting inline code spans with placeholders.

2. llms-txt.ts: extractInlineCode regex matched across newlines,
   pairing code fence backticks with inline ones and capturing
   entire code blocks as "tags". Fixed by stripping fences first
   and excluding newlines from the match pattern.

Re-scraped all libraries to regenerate clean tags.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add safe eval launcher with explicit PID tracking per batch

Avoids nohup `wait` returning early by tracking PIDs explicitly.
Previous launcher caused all 3 batches to start simultaneously,
leading to GPU contention and 17/24 model failures.

v2.2.0: Fix critical bugs, robust undo, bundle Font Awesome locally

- Create new RegExp instances per pattern to prevent shared lastIndex bugs
- Rewrite handleUndo with per-placeholder replacement for rich text editors

- Replace cache hash with FNV-1a for better distribution

commit.message:
refactor(dorfkoenig): UI component system, design tokens, and accessibility polish

Build reusable UI primitives (Badge, StatusPill, IconButton, ConfirmStrip,
FormGroup, EmptyState), extend the design token system with semantic colors
and type scale, then systematically re-skin all ~47 components to use tokens
instead of hardcoded values. Add focus-trap, aria-live regions, and
visually-hidden headings for accessibility.

- Extend global.css with badge/status semantic colors, type scale, --font-article
- Fix --radius-xl bug (was identical to --radius-md)
- Create 6 UI primitives in components/ui/primitives/
- Re-skin ScoutCard with primitives (CSS 274→~115 lines)
- Re-skin Feed/Compose panel (UnitList, SelectionBar, DraftSlideOver, etc.)
- Apply tokens to ExecutionCard, ScoutModal, UploadModal, Layout, filters
- Fix ScoutWizardStep1 criteria color bug (indigo→coral)
- Align all 11 Bajour components with token system (zero hardcoded hex)
- Shrink styles.css from 611→~220 lines (dead CSS removed)
- Add focus-trap action to ScoutModal, UploadModal, DraftSlideOver
- Add aria-live, aria-required, visually-hidden h1s for a11y
- Replace plain empty states with EmptyState component across all routes
- Shared Card gains interactive prop with hover lift

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add ANSI A92.2 Major Structural Load Test template

- Created load_test_structural.json with 5 sections (31 questions)
- Based on ANSI A92.2 Section 8.2.5 and 4.5 specifications
- Pre-test inspection for structural integrity
- Level surface test at 1.5x rated capacity
- Slope test at 1.33x rated capacity on 5° slope
- Post-test inspection for permanent deformation
- Test documentation with service age tracking
- Selectable as optional test module (kind: "test")
- Required after 15 years, then every 10 years

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix bug: kitchen safety room data bleeding across all rooms

handleSelectRoom was fetching getTodayKitchenSafetyCheck by nursery only
(no room filter), causing one room's completed sections to appear in all rooms.
Now loads only from room-specific localStorage key.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add upscaling retry for barcode detection on small regions

ZXing can fail to detect barcodes in small cropped regions where
barcode modules are only a few pixels wide. When the initial decode
attempts fail and the image is under 500px in either dimension,
retry at 2x and 3x scale with bicubic interpolation. Bounding boxes
are scaled back to original coordinates.

commit.message:
fix: XDP host-inbound-all drops unknown services, causing cluster split-brain

xdp_policy.c host-inbound fallback checked `hif != 0 && (flags & hif)`
but host_inbound_flag() returns 0 for unknown services (e.g. heartbeat
UDP 4784). When host_inbound_flags == HOST_INBOUND_ALL, this incorrectly
denied the packet. xdp_forward.c already had the correct check
(`flags != HOST_INBOUND_ALL`).

After `bpfrxd cleanup` during deploy, conntrack sessions are wiped.
New heartbeat packets hit xdp_policy as new flows, got denied by the
broken check, and both nodes declared peer lost — split-brain.

Also fixes isPeerInterface() to use FPC slot→node-id mapping for
cross-node monitor interface proxy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Team-wide review stats, latest-reviewer cards, full owner dropdown

- Everyone sees the same shared view regardless of selected user
- Proposals owner dropdown now includes all team members (Suselle, Kathy, Michelle, Elaine)

docs: overhaul guide documentation for beginners (#83)

* docs: overhaul guide documentation — beginner-friendly, 3-mode structure

Restructure guides from scratch for first-time developers:

Introduction:
- Polish "What is Durably?" with 3-mode table
- New Quick Start: single Node.js file, runs in 2 minutes
- Rewrite Core Concepts: simpler, progressive explanation

Tutorials (progressive):
- Server Mode: batch processing, cron, CLI (replaces background-sync)
- Fullstack Mode: React + SSE + dashboard (merges getting-started + csv-import)
- SPA Mode: browser OPFS, offline-capable (replaces offline-app)

Patterns (from issue #77):
- Error Handling & Retry: failure patterns, idempotency, partial failures
- Auth & Multi-Tenant: authenticate, onTrigger, onRunAccess, scopeRuns
- Deployment Guide: mode comparison, decision flowchart, migration paths

Also update: sidebar, READMEs, skills, homepage link.

Closes #77

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

- Auth page: focused on authentication with React Router, Next.js, Hono examples

- 0단계: TObjectRange 전체 수집 → TArray<UUWManagerBase*> Ordered
- 1단계: Ordered.Sort(GetInitPriority 오름차순) → Init() 순서 보장
- 2단계: 동일 정렬 순서로 PostInit() 일괄 호출

blocks all mic access at HTTP level. Changed to `microphone=(self)`.

SpeechRecognition). Replaced canvas waveform with CSS-only equalizer.

fix: address 5 bugs found during E2E pairing/content pipeline test

- BUG #1 (CRITICAL): Add device status catch-up on dashboard WebSocket
  connect so devices show correct online/offline state immediately
- BUG #2 (LOW): Add countdown timer to pairing screen in vizora-tv
  (HTML element + startPairingCountdown/stopPairingCountdown methods)
- BUG #3 (MEDIUM): Set lastHeartbeat on pairing completion so dashboard
  shows "Last Seen" instead of "Never" for newly paired devices
- BUG #4 (HIGH): Allow free-tier users to perform write operations
  (pairing, content upload/push) after trial expires — was blocking
  all writes with 403 "subscription inactive"
- BUG #5 (LOW): Change misleading "Offline" label to "Live sync off"
  on content page when WebSocket disconnects — content management
  still works via REST

Also updates subscription guard tests with free-tier test cases and
includes the full E2E test report in bug-reports/.

fix(firestore): update rules to support V2 onboarding document IDs

The V2 onboarding system uses eval_v2_{userId} document IDs while the
rules only supported eval_{userId}. Added isOwnEvaluation() helper that
checks both formats and updated create/read/update rules accordingly.

Fix build-jar.sh: use /usr/libexec/java_home on macOS, numeric version check

readlink -f doesn't resolve through macOS /usr/bin/java stub, causing
the script to hang. Use /usr/libexec/java_home instead. Also replace
hardcoded version grep pattern with numeric >= 17 comparison so future
JDK versions (25+) work automatically.
